# Brand Register

Use this register when a Flutter native surface carries the brand message: onboarding, launch screens, campaign-like product education, store-style feature pages, celebratory moments, or other screens where impression is the product.

The bar is distinctiveness, but native constraints still matter. The screen must respect safe areas, text scaling, accessibility, gestures, platform navigation, and performance.

## Flutter Brand Priorities

- Let one strong idea own the screen: typography, image, color, motion, or interaction.
- Use ThemeData and component themes so expressive surfaces still fit the app.
- Keep onboarding and education skippable when users are trying to reach a task.
- Treat imagery and animation as real assets, not placeholders.
- Use platform transitions and gestures intentionally.

## Brand Bans

- A marketing web layout pasted into a native screen.
- Decorative glass, blur, shadow, and gradients without a specific purpose.
- Huge fixed text that clips under text scaling.
- Long copy blocks that ignore thumb reach and reading context.
- Motion that blocks entry into the app.
